ops.text: codex exec workspace-write sandbox denies ALL .git writes (objects, index.lock, packed-refs.lock, refs/jj) — so any runbook that has codex itself commit/push (ccx vcs ship, git commit, even 'jj log' when it lazily imports git refs) dies with 'Operation not permitted'. This silently broke a 39-repo fan-out on 2026-07-17: renders and tree edits succeeded, every ship was refused, leaving 27 repos wired-but-uncommitted. The 2026-07-16 remigration shipped from codex fine, so either its wrapper ran a different sandbox mode or codex changed between versions. Rule of thumb until resolved: codex edits, Claude ships — keep VCS mutations outside the codex sandbox, or find/verify a sandbox_workspace_write git-writes toggle before trusting codex with ship steps.
